methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Pre-Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $6M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Limelight Steel is a pre-seed stage climate and energy company founded by Olivia Dippo and Andy Zhao that has raised $6M to date. The climate and energy sector includes one public comparable (LNZA) and eight private peers, with notable private companies including TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape.

The stage-progression method is the applicable triangulation approach for this valuation. Using total capital raised ($6M) as a proxy for current implied value and applying a typical pre-seed to next-stage step-up multiplier of 2.5x yields an implied valuation of $15M. The peer-median method is not applicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow climate and energy peer set; while 19 same-sector peers exist broadly, none of the tight filters produced three or more datapoints necessary for reliable median anchoring.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $13M to $17M, with a median point of $15M. This range reflects a single-method triangulation and carries material uncertainty around the appropriate step-up multiple for frontier energy technologies and the absence of direct peer valuation anchors. Key sensitivities include technology differentiation relative to the peer universe and near-term funding environment conditions for pre-seed climate ventures.
